22029 SATICOY STREET, CANOGA PARK, CA 91303

Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.28 3.90 4.23 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.41 0.68 0.41 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.27 0.87 1.25 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.60 2.35 2.57 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.68 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.10 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.90
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.30
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.86
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
26.1%
annual
RN turnover
12.5%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.38
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.00
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-27 32 23 9 176 1 176
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-08 17 10 9 80 1 80
Total weighted health score
152.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
1
last cycle

Deficiencies (63)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

63 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-08-25 2025-09-09
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2025-08-25 2025-09-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2025-08-25 2025-09-09
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2025-08-08 2025-09-25
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-07-25 2025-08-15
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-07-25 2025-08-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-07-25 2025-08-15
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. E 2025-06-04 2025-07-02
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-06-04 2025-07-02
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-02-27 2025-03-20
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-06-20 2024-06-28
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-04-18 2024-04-26
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-04-18 2024-04-26
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-08 2024-03-20
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-09-28 2023-10-11
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2023-08-01 2023-08-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-06-28 2023-07-13
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2021-08-06 2021-08-30

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description

Deficiencies 0809 — description Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests.
